generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T FERRUM TARTARICUM. Authority. E. W. Berridge, M.D., U. S. Med. Invest., New Ser., vol. iv, 1876, p. 574, Mr --- took 15 grains in one dose. ► Uninterrupted sensation, as if an immense load were on entire upper portion of head, causing a gloomy apprehensiveness of congestive apoplexy. It lasted two or three days, and was removed by a dose of Nux 1 (after two days). ► Heat from, or heat at cardiac orifice of stomach, lasting twelve hours; not affected by food or drink (after two hours). keynotes.text: Berridge has recorded these two symptoms caused by a 15-grain dose: (1) Uninterrupted sensation as if an immense load were on entire upper portion of head, causing a gloomy apprehensiveness of congestive apoplexy (lasted three days, removed by a dose of Nux 1). (2) Heat from or heat at cardiac orifice of stomach lasting 12 hours; unaffected by food or drink. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main
